---
title: Contributing
---

## Contributing to Responsible Use of Generative AI

Contributions are welcome for:

- content corrections
- improved teaching activities
- updated institutional or policy references
- clearer examples of prompt design, validation, and documentation
- accessibility and usability improvements

## How to Contribute

1. Open an issue describing the change.
2. If editing content directly, use a focused branch and clear commit messages.
3. Submit a pull request with a concise summary of what changed and why.

## What Makes a Good Contribution

- Keep the material practical and judgement-focused.
- Prefer source-grounded examples over abstract claims.
- Avoid hype and keep governance language concrete.
- Flag policy, tool, or institutional references that may have changed.
- If a change affects delivery, note the implications for learners or instructors.

## Licensing and Attribution

By contributing to this course, you agree that your contribution can be published under the terms described in [LICENSE.qmd](LICENSE.qmd).

If you reuse outside material, include enough information for that source to be credited and checked properly.

## Content Principles

- Keep material practical and judgement-focused.
- Prefer source-grounded examples over abstract claims.
- Avoid tool hype and keep governance language concrete.
- Ensure references to policy or process remain current.

## Project Policies

- [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.qmd)
- [License](LICENSE.qmd)
